agentMessage.excerpt:
Review checklist → needsFix
✓  Build completed — the implementor finished in 24 round(s)
✓  Change is visible — 3 file(s) changed: internal/discovery/detect.go, internal/engine/language_tiers.go, internal/engine/language_tiers_test.go
✓  Changed the deliverable's target files — expected ≥1 of: internal/engine/language_tiers.go, internal/engine/language_tiers_test.go
✓  Verification passed — the project verify command was GREEN
✓  Work committed — committed locally on the build branch
0.30  Implements the approved plan — Requeue guidance (authoritative) says 'do NOT create a second copy under internal/engine/' and to extend existing internal/language_tiers/ — diff creates new internal/engine/language_tiers.go and _test.go in the forbidden location instead.
0.50  Minimal and scoped — Only 3 files changed and the substance (registry, Tier enum, 3 tests) matches plan intent, but the files are placed in the wrong package, violating the requeue directive that explicitly forbade internal/engine/ placement.
0.90  No test-gaming / faking — KnownLanguages() iterates the real extToLanguage map (not hardcoded), all 3 tests assert genuine behavior with no deleted/weakened tests or forced expected values.
0.40  Safe to advance — Creating a second tier system in internal/engine/ when internal/language_tiers/ already exists per #29 introduces a duplicate registry surface that could diverge and confuse consumers — a code-organization regression risk.
0.95  Judge confidence — at or above the confidence bar

Reviewer independently re-ran the project verification (go build -o build/bin/certify ./cmd/certify/ && go test ./... -count=1): GREEN

Reviewer:
- Requeue guidance (authoritative) forbids creating files under internal/engine/ — must extend existing internal/language_tiers/ package instead. Diff adds internal/engine/language_tiers.go and _test.go in direct violation.
- The existing internal/language_tiers/ package from #29 already has a tier classifier; this diff creates a competing/duplicate system rather than extending the established one.
- aggregate score 0.525 below threshold 0.600
